description: Concurrent use significantly increases risk of major bleeding events including GI hemorrhage
mechanism: Both drugs inhibit hemostasis: warfarin inhibits vitamin K-dependent clotting factors while aspirin irreversibly inhibits platelet COX-1

description: NSAIDs increase anticoagulant effect and risk of GI bleeding when combined with warfarin
mechanism: Ibuprofen inhibits platelet function and may displace warfarin from protein binding sites

description: Metronidazole inhibits warfarin metabolism leading to increased INR and bleeding risk
mechanism: Metronidazole inhibits CYP2C9 which metabolizes S-warfarin

description: Risk of lactic acidosis when metformin is combined with iodinated contrast media
mechanism: Contrast media can cause acute kidney injury reducing metformin clearance leading to accumulation
source: ACR Manual on Contrast Media; PMID:26541880

description: Excessive alcohol intake with metformin increases risk of lactic acidosis
mechanism: Alcohol potentiates the effect of metformin on lactate metabolism

description: Furosemide may increase metformin plasma levels; metformin may decrease furosemide levels
mechanism: Competitive renal tubular secretion between the two drugs

description: Antacids containing aluminum or magnesium significantly reduce ciprofloxacin absorption
mechanism: Metal cations form chelation complexes with ciprofloxacin reducing bioavailability by up to 90%

description: Ciprofloxacin inhibits theophylline metabolism leading to toxic theophylline levels
mechanism: Ciprofloxacin inhibits CYP1A2 which is the primary enzyme metabolizing theophylline

description: Ciprofloxacin may enhance anticoagulant effect of warfarin
mechanism: Inhibition of CYP1A2 and possible alteration of gut flora reducing vitamin K synthesis

description: Amoxicillin may increase methotrexate toxicity through reduced renal clearance
mechanism: Penicillins compete with methotrexate for renal tubular secretion

description: Amoxicillin may enhance anticoagulant effect by altering gut flora
mechanism: Disruption of vitamin K-producing intestinal bacteria
source: OpenFDA amoxicillin label; clinical case reports

description: Omeprazole significantly reduces antiplatelet effect of clopidogrel increasing cardiovascular risk
mechanism: Omeprazole inhibits CYP2C19 which is required to convert clopidogrel prodrug to active metabolite

description: Omeprazole may increase methotrexate serum levels
mechanism: Inhibition of renal H+/K+ ATPase may reduce methotrexate elimination

description: Omeprazole reduces absorption of oral iron supplements
mechanism: Gastric acid suppression reduces conversion of ferric to ferrous iron needed for absorption

description: Clarithromycin increases atorvastatin exposure leading to increased risk of rhabdomyolysis
mechanism: Clarithromycin strongly inhibits CYP3A4 which metabolizes atorvastatin

description: Large amounts of grapefruit juice increase atorvastatin levels
mechanism: Grapefruit contains furanocoumarins that inhibit intestinal CYP3A4

description: Combination increases risk of myopathy and rhabdomyolysis
mechanism: Gemfibrozil inhibits OATP1B1 transporter and glucuronidation of statins

description: Amlodipine increases simvastatin exposure; simvastatin dose should not exceed 20mg
mechanism: Amlodipine inhibits CYP3A4 reducing simvastatin metabolism

description: Combined use can cause life-threatening hyperkalemia
mechanism: ACE inhibitors reduce aldosterone-mediated potassium excretion; adding potassium worsens retention

description: Dual RAAS blockade significantly increases risk of hyperkalemia and renal impairment
mechanism: Both drugs independently increase serum potassium through different mechanisms

description: NSAIDs may reduce antihypertensive effect and increase risk of renal impairment
mechanism: NSAIDs reduce renal prostaglandin synthesis which is important for maintaining renal perfusion in ACE inhibitor-treated patients

description: Concurrent use increases risk of GI bleeding and may reduce cardioprotective effect of aspirin
mechanism: Both are COX inhibitors; diclofenac may compete with aspirin at the COX-1 binding site

description: Diclofenac increases lithium serum levels by reducing renal clearance
mechanism: NSAIDs reduce prostaglandin-mediated renal lithium excretion

description: Diclofenac reduces methotrexate renal elimination increasing toxicity risk
mechanism: NSAIDs inhibit prostaglandin-mediated renal blood flow and compete for tubular secretion

description: Regular paracetamol use may modestly increase INR in warfarin-treated patients
mechanism: Paracetamol metabolite may inhibit vitamin K-dependent carboxylase

description: Chronic alcohol use with paracetamol significantly increases risk of hepatotoxicity
mechanism: Alcohol induces CYP2E1 increasing production of hepatotoxic NAPQI metabolite while depleting glutathione

description: Isoniazid may increase risk of paracetamol hepatotoxicity
mechanism: Isoniazid induces CYP2E1 increasing NAPQI production

description: Combination can cause severe bradycardia and heart block
mechanism: Both drugs suppress AV node conduction through different mechanisms

description: Beta-blockers may mask symptoms of hypoglycemia and prolong hypoglycemic episodes
mechanism: Beta-blockers blunt tachycardia and tremor warning signs; may impair glycogenolysis

description: Fluoxetine inhibits metoprolol metabolism leading to increased beta-blockade
mechanism: Fluoxetine is a potent CYP2D6 inhibitor; metoprolol is a CYP2D6 substrate

description: Combination can cause serotonin syndrome which can be fatal
mechanism: Both drugs increase serotonergic activity; combination causes excessive serotonin accumulation
source: OpenFDA fluoxetine label section 4; FDA black box warning

description: Increased risk of serotonin syndrome and seizures
mechanism: Both drugs increase serotonergic activity; tramadol also lowers seizure threshold

description: Fluoxetine may increase warfarin plasma levels and bleeding risk
mechanism: Fluoxetine inhibits CYP2C9 and is highly protein-bound displacing warfarin

description: Combination increases risk of QT prolongation and torsades de pointes
mechanism: Both drugs independently prolong QT interval; additive effect on cardiac repolarization

description: Azithromycin may enhance anticoagulant effect of warfarin
mechanism: Alteration of gut flora reducing vitamin K synthesis

description: Azithromycin may increase digoxin levels by altering gut flora
mechanism: Reduced bacterial inactivation of digoxin in the gut

description: Amiodarone increases digoxin levels by approximately 70%
mechanism: Amiodarone inhibits P-glycoprotein mediated renal and intestinal digoxin clearance

description: Verapamil increases digoxin serum levels by 50-75%
mechanism: Inhibition of P-glycoprotein and reduction of renal digoxin clearance

description: Furosemide-induced hypokalemia increases sensitivity to digoxin toxicity
mechanism: Potassium depletion enhances binding of digoxin to Na+/K+ ATPase

description: Omeprazole reduces clopidogrel antiplatelet efficacy
mechanism: CYP2C19 inhibition prevents conversion of clopidogrel prodrug to active metabolite

description: Dual antiplatelet therapy increases bleeding risk but may be indicated
mechanism: Additive inhibition of platelet aggregation via different pathways

description: Fluconazole significantly increases phenytoin levels causing toxicity
mechanism: Fluconazole inhibits CYP2C9 and CYP2C19 which metabolize phenytoin

description: Complex bidirectional interaction altering levels of both drugs
mechanism: Both drugs induce hepatic enzymes; phenytoin induces carbamazepine metabolism and vice versa

description: Folic acid may reduce phenytoin levels; phenytoin reduces folate absorption
mechanism: Folic acid may increase phenytoin metabolism; phenytoin inhibits folate absorption

description: Erythromycin significantly increases carbamazepine levels causing toxicity
mechanism: Erythromycin strongly inhibits CYP3A4 which is the primary metabolic pathway for carbamazepine

description: Carbamazepine reduces effectiveness of oral contraceptives leading to contraceptive failure
mechanism: Carbamazepine induces CYP3A4 increasing metabolism of ethinyl estradiol and progestins
source: OpenFDA label; WHO guidance on contraception

description: Grapefruit juice increases carbamazepine bioavailability
mechanism: Inhibition of intestinal CYP3A4 by furanocoumarins in grapefruit

description: Combined use significantly increases risk of GI ulceration and bleeding
mechanism: Corticosteroids impair mucosal defense; NSAIDs inhibit protective prostaglandin synthesis

description: Corticosteroids cause hyperglycemia counteracting diabetes medications
mechanism: Glucocorticoids increase hepatic gluconeogenesis and reduce peripheral glucose uptake
source: OpenFDA label; clinical dose adjustment usually needed

description: Corticosteroids may alter warfarin response unpredictably
mechanism: Variable effects on coagulation factors and warfarin metabolism
source: OpenFDA label; frequent INR monitoring recommended

description: Alcohol can cause severe prolonged hypoglycemia in insulin-treated patients
mechanism: Alcohol inhibits hepatic gluconeogenesis; impairs counter-regulatory hormone response

description: Beta-blockers may mask hypoglycemia symptoms and prolong episodes
mechanism: Blunting of tachycardia and tremor; impaired glycogenolysis

description: Fluoroquinolones can cause both hypo- and hyperglycemia in diabetic patients
mechanism: Direct effect on pancreatic beta-cell insulin secretion

description: Calcium reduces levothyroxine absorption when taken together
mechanism: Calcium forms insoluble complexes with levothyroxine in the GI tract

description: Iron significantly reduces levothyroxine absorption
mechanism: Iron forms chelation complexes with levothyroxine reducing bioavailability
source: OpenFDA label; separate by at least 4 hours

description: PPIs may reduce levothyroxine absorption by increasing gastric pH
mechanism: Levothyroxine absorption is partially pH-dependent in the stomach

description: Combination increases risk of serotonin syndrome and seizures
mechanism: Both drugs increase serotonergic activity; tramadol also lowers seizure threshold

description: Risk of serotonin syndrome and seizures; combination contraindicated
mechanism: Tramadol inhibits serotonin and norepinephrine reuptake; MAOIs prevent monoamine breakdown

description: Carbamazepine reduces tramadol analgesic effect
mechanism: Carbamazepine induces CYP3A4 increasing tramadol metabolism to inactive metabolites

description: Antacids reduce doxycycline absorption significantly
mechanism: Divalent and trivalent cations chelate with tetracyclines forming non-absorbable complexes

description: Iron significantly reduces doxycycline bioavailability
mechanism: Metal ion chelation forming non-absorbable complexes
source: OpenFDA label; separate administration by 2-3 hours

description: Doxycycline may theoretically reduce OCP effectiveness
mechanism: Possible alteration of gut flora affecting enterohepatic recycling of estrogen
source: OpenFDA label; evidence is weak but listed

description: Risk of hyperkalemia similar to ACE inhibitors
mechanism: ARBs reduce aldosterone secretion decreasing renal potassium excretion

description: Losartan increases lithium levels by reducing renal clearance
mechanism: Reduced prostaglandin-mediated renal lithium excretion similar to ACE inhibitors

description: NSAIDs reduce antihypertensive effect and increase renal risk
mechanism: Reduction in prostaglandin-mediated renal blood flow
source: OpenFDA label; similar to ACE inhibitor interaction

description: Combination increases risk of respiratory depression and death
mechanism: Additive CNS depressant effects

description: Antacids reduce gabapentin bioavailability by approximately 20%
mechanism: Altered GI pH and absorption kinetics

description: Combination significantly increases risk of respiratory depression
mechanism: Additive CNS depression; both drugs depress respiratory drive

description: Alcohol enhances CNS depressant effects of pregabalin
mechanism: Additive sedation and psychomotor impairment

description: Ranitidine may cause modest increase in warfarin effect
mechanism: Minor inhibition of warfarin metabolism; less significant than cimetidine

description: Ranitidine reduces ketoconazole absorption by raising gastric pH
mechanism: Ketoconazole requires acidic gastric pH for dissolution and absorption

description: Risk of fatal serotonin syndrome; combination is absolutely contraindicated
mechanism: Excessive serotonergic stimulation from combined reuptake inhibition and MAO inhibition
source: OpenFDA sertraline label section 4; FDA black box warning

description: Increased risk of serotonin syndrome
mechanism: Both drugs inhibit serotonin reuptake

description: Sertraline may increase bleeding risk with warfarin
mechanism: CYP enzyme inhibition and serotonin-mediated platelet effects

description: May reduce clopidogrel efficacy though less than omeprazole
mechanism: Weaker CYP2C19 inhibition compared to omeprazole
source: PMID:20083682; considered safer PPI option with clopidogrel

description: PPIs may increase methotrexate levels
mechanism: Inhibition of renal H+/K+ ATPase may reduce methotrexate elimination

description: Combination increases risk of rhabdomyolysis significantly
mechanism: Clarithromycin strongly inhibits CYP3A4 and P-glycoprotein
source: OpenFDA labels; simvastatin contraindicated with clarithromycin

description: Can cause fatal colchicine toxicity especially in renal impairment
mechanism: Clarithromycin inhibits CYP3A4 and P-glycoprotein reducing colchicine clearance

description: Clarithromycin increases digoxin levels significantly
mechanism: Inhibition of P-glycoprotein and altered gut flora bacterial digoxin inactivation

description: NSAIDs reduce methotrexate renal clearance increasing toxicity risk
mechanism: Inhibition of prostaglandin-mediated renal blood flow and tubular secretion competition

description: Trimethoprim increases methotrexate toxicity through antifolate synergy
mechanism: Both drugs inhibit dihydrofolate reductase; additive bone marrow suppression

description: Proton pump inhibitors may increase methotrexate serum levels
mechanism: Inhibition of renal H+/K+ ATPase transporters reducing methotrexate elimination

description: Combination causes severe life-threatening hypotension; absolutely contraindicated
mechanism: Both drugs increase cGMP-mediated vasodilation; additive hypotensive effect
source: OpenFDA sildenafil label section 4; FDA black box

description: Risk of symptomatic hypotension especially with initial doses
mechanism: Additive vasodilatory effects leading to orthostatic hypotension
source: OpenFDA sildenafil label; dose adjustment required

description: Ritonavir significantly increases sildenafil levels
mechanism: Ritonavir inhibits CYP3A4 the primary metabolic pathway for sildenafil
source: OpenFDA label; maximum sildenafil 25mg per 48 hours

description: Additive QT prolongation risk with other QT-prolonging medications
mechanism: Amiodarone blocks multiple cardiac ion channels; additive effects with similar drugs
source: OpenFDA amiodarone label; ECG monitoring required

description: Amiodarone inhibits warfarin metabolism increasing INR significantly
mechanism: Inhibition of CYP2C9 and CYP1A2; effect can persist for months after amiodarone discontinuation
source: OpenFDA label; reduce warfarin dose by 30-50%

description: Amiodarone increases simvastatin levels increasing rhabdomyolysis risk
mechanism: Inhibition of CYP3A4; simvastatin dose should not exceed 20mg
source: OpenFDA simvastatin label; FDA dose limitation

description: Combination is contraindicated due to extreme rhabdomyolysis risk
mechanism: Ketoconazole is a potent CYP3A4 inhibitor causing massive statin accumulation

description: Risk of fatal cardiac arrhythmias; combination contraindicated
mechanism: CYP3A4 inhibition increases cisapride levels causing QT prolongation

description: Rifampin dramatically reduces OCP effectiveness causing contraceptive failure
mechanism: Rifampin is one of the most potent CYP3A4 inducers known
source: OpenFDA rifampin label; WHO contraception guidance

description: Rifampin dramatically reduces warfarin levels
mechanism: Potent induction of CYP2C9 CYP3A4 and CYP1A2
source: OpenFDA rifampin label; requires large warfarin dose increases

description: Rifampin reduces protease inhibitor levels to sub-therapeutic range
mechanism: Massive CYP3A4 induction; can cause HIV treatment failure

description: NSAIDs increase lithium levels by 15-30% risking toxicity
mechanism: Reduced renal prostaglandin-mediated lithium excretion

description: ACE inhibitors may increase lithium levels
mechanism: Reduced renal lithium clearance through hemodynamic effects
source: OpenFDA label; frequent lithium monitoring required

description: Thiazide diuretics significantly increase lithium levels
mechanism: Sodium depletion causes compensatory lithium reabsorption in proximal tubule
source: OpenFDA lithium label; thiazides most problematic

description: Significant risk of life-threatening hyperkalemia
mechanism: Both drugs independently increase serum potassium; additive effect
source: OpenFDA labels; RALES trial showed benefit with monitoring

description: Combination frequently causes dangerous hyperkalemia
mechanism: Spironolactone blocks aldosterone-mediated K+ excretion; exogenous K+ adds to retention
source: OpenFDA spironolactone label; combination generally avoided

description: NSAIDs reduce diuretic effect and increase hyperkalemia risk
mechanism: Reduced prostaglandin-mediated renal blood flow and potassium excretion

description: Valproic acid doubles lamotrigine half-life requiring dose reduction
mechanism: Inhibition of lamotrigine glucuronidation by valproic acid
source: OpenFDA lamotrigine label; slow titration required

description: Carbapenems reduce valproic acid levels by 60-80% causing seizure breakthrough
mechanism: Carbapenems increase valproic acid glucuronidation and may inhibit absorption
source: OpenFDA label; combination should be avoided

description: Aspirin displaces valproic acid from protein binding and inhibits metabolism
mechanism: Protein binding competition and inhibition of beta-oxidation
source: OpenFDA valproic acid label; monitor free VPA levels
